title: YOAH
full_description: YOAH — moon and hope in Japanese — takes a young girl lost in the underworld on a journey of dark gothic fantasy, infused with Japanese aesthetics and propelled by state-of-the-art digital visual effects. Directed by Mizuke Shinigawa and Yusaku Mochizuki, the show uses diabolo, silks, trapeze and aerial work in a silent spectacle shaped by the Japanese principles of Ma (pause) and Yohaku (blank space).
ai_summary: Japanese contemporary circus company YOAH blends aerial silks, diabolo and trapeze with cinematic digital visuals and Japanese aesthetics in a silent, gothic underworld fantasy — following a girl in a hoodie pursued by four masked figures through a world of darkness and light.
